prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Download the AE101 content tarball to `~/Downloads/agents-102-content.tar.gz` using cur

risks_fired.evidence: Single-action Bash fetch; brief opener ('I'll download that now') possible in ~20% of runs but non-disruptive on a housekeeping step.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No edit needed; preamble on a setup-only prompt does not affect learning outcome.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: I'm starting a six-module training called Agentic Engineering 101. Walk these in

risks_fired.evidence: Five-step numbered task; 'no preamble' suppressor present but confirmatory opener ('I'll work through these in order…') persists in ~40% of runs at this complexity level.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Add body primer immediately after the fence: 'Claude will narrate before acting; skim past the opening and look for the step number.'

risks_fired.evidence: CARVE-OUT APPLIED. Step 4 elicitation has two body callouts satisfying the carve-out shape: pre-prompt names failure mode + recovery ('push back if it dumps all four criteria at once'); post-prompt names the AskUserQuestion tool + one-at-a-time option + student agency ('Your call.'). Downgraded from med to low.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Carve-out satisfied — no prompt edit required.

risks_fired.evidence: Step 3 installs to ~/.claude/skills/ without explicit overwrite instruction; pause-to-ask fires on skill-name collision, rare for first-time installs.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Optional: add 'overwrite if exists' to step 3 for repeat-run safety.

risks_fired.evidence: 'Report success on each step and tell me what you did' invites self-summary; Claude may claim install confirmed when path check was approximate. Non-load-bearing for prework's teaching goal.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Not blocking at low confidence. If cohort shows install-path confusion, add explicit path-verification: 'Show me the full absolute path to each installed SKILL.md.'

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Use the AskUserQuestion tool for speeding up screening and making suggestions. One cand

risks_fired.evidence: One-liner tool-switch command; 'Sure, switching to AskUserQuestion' opener possible but compliance is near-certain and disruption minimal.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No edit needed; one-liner compliance commands have negligible preamble impact.
